body_txt: A common subtype of the Tag Team — a group of characters that take turns fighting — is a twin tag team, usually identical or quasi-identical twins. Not only may they function as a team better than non-twin pairings or use Twin Telepathy , but also take advantage of their similar looks by trading places with others unaware. As the Tag Team fighting format was derived from a common professional wrestling gimmick match , so do Tag Team Twins often crop up in wrestling duos, either being real-life twins or performing as twins in Kayfabe . Swapping places during a match behind the ref's back , allowing the fresh sibling to take the injured one's place, is a classic heel tactic. Appearances in Video Games may consist of the twins being swap fighters or the player character(s) having to fight them as sequential bosses . As a subtrope of Tag Team , taking turns is a mandatory component. While the currently inactive twin may function as an Assist Character or help by performing a Combination Attack , twins fighting or being fought simultaneously falls under Sibling Team and related tropes. Compare Twin Switch , a plot where twins or Identical Strangers trade places or pretend to be each other. See also Superior Twin Teamwork for when the twin team is shown to be far better than all other competitors because of their superior coordination. Has nothing to do with the Twin Threesome Fantasy .
